title: Run compatibility on main pushes and document green-commit release sequencing

sources.reason: Current workflow only runs on pull_request/manual and does not re-prove main push commits.
Current smoke workflow is the existing repository-level release and governance gate.
Regression coverage for push triggers and the documented commit-bound release sequence.
Release evidence and immutable source identity are documented here.
This contract declares the release CI hardening scope.

riskAssessment.reason: Workflow triggers and immutable release evidence affect public release trust.

executionDecision.reason: Repository workflow and release validator changes are implementable in this Work Item.

preReviewWarnings: Review workflow trigger semantics, release SHA immutability, and tag-vs-worktree evidence separation.

checkpointPolicy.reason: Record at least one checkpoint before finishing to reduce mid-task drift.

acceptance: Compatibility CI runs for pull requests, main pushes, and manual dispatch without weakening existing matrix coverage.
The release documentation states that the release tag must equal the commit SHA verified by both smoke and compatibility workflows before publication.
Regression tests cover the compatibility workflow trigger configuration and the immutable historical tag rule.
Release documentation describes the immutable commit/tag relationship and the PR-first release sequence.
guidelines: Release evidence must be derived from the inspected tag or explicit source commit, never from the current worktree by accident.
Workflow changes must preserve pinned third-party action SHAs and least-privilege permissions.
Public release claims must remain explainable from checked-in metadata and CI evidence.

scenarioCoverage.scenario: release tag is created only after both required workflows succeed
scenarioCoverage.reason: GitHub platform Ruleset enforcement must be configured and observed by a repository administrator.

intent.problem: v0.5.24 was tagged from a commit whose smoke CI failed, and compatibility CI does not rerun on direct main pushes, so release sequencing lacks a repository-level reminder to bind a future tag to fresh evidence.
constraints: Do not move or rewrite v0.5.24.
Keep historical release evidence validation fail-closed.
Keep third-party GitHub Actions pinned by immutable SHA.
intent.rationale: Make the next release sequence mechanically bind CI evidence, tag identity, and published release metadata before publication.

restrictedWriteApproval.reason: User explicitly authorized execution of the accepted PR-first release hardening plan; workflow trigger changes remain limited to this Contract scope.

rollbackNote: Revert this Work Item diff and restore related tests and docs.
